claim: Knowledge is a @dataclass extending RemoteKnowledge
quote: @dataclass class Knowledge(RemoteKnowledge): """Knowledge class"""

claim: Knowledge constructor exposes vector_db, contents_db, max_results=10, readers, content_sources, isolate_vector_search
quote: vector_db: Optional[Any] = None; contents_db: Optional[Union[BaseDb, AsyncBaseDb]] = None; max_results: int = 10; readers: Optional[Dict[str, Reader]]; content_sources: Optional[List[BaseStorageConfig]]; isolate_vector_search: bool = False

claim: Primary ingest verbs are insert/ainsert; no add_content
quote: def insert(self, name=None, description=None, path=None, url=None, text_content=None, metadata=None, topics=None, remote_content=None, reader=None, include=None, exclude=None, upsert=True, skip_if_exists=False, auth=None) -> None

claim: search signature: query, max_results, filters, search_type → List[Document]
quote: def search(self, query: str, max_results: Optional[int] = None, filters: Optional[Union[Dict[str, Any], List[FilterExpr]]] = None, search_type: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Document]

claim: SearchType enum has exactly three values
quote: class SearchType(str, Enum): vector = "vector"; keyword = "keyword"; hybrid = "hybrid"

claim: 18 vector DB backends in source: cassandra, chroma, clickhouse, couchbase, lancedb, langchaindb, lightrag, llamaindex, milvus, mongodb, pgvector, pineconedb, qdrant, redis, singlestore, surrealdb, upstashdb, weaviate
quote: Directory listing of libs/agno/agno/vectordb/

claim: 19 embedder modules in source
quote: aws_bedrock, azure_openai, cohere, fastembed, fireworks, google, huggingface, jina, langdb, mistral, nebius, ollama, openai, openai_like, sentence_transformer, together, vllm, voyageai + base

claim: 18 reader modules + reader_factory
quote: arxiv, csv, docling, docx, excel, field_labeled_csv, firecrawl, json, llms_txt, markdown, pdf, pptx, s3, tavily, text, web_search, website, wikipedia, youtube

claim: 8 chunking strategies
quote: AGENTIC_CHUNKER, CODE_CHUNKER, DOCUMENT_CHUNKER, RECURSIVE_CHUNKER, SEMANTIC_CHUNKER, FIXED_SIZE_CHUNKER, ROW_CHUNKER, MARKDOWN_CHUNKER

claim: ContentStatus enum: PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED
quote: class ContentStatus(str, Enum): PROCESSING = "processing"; COMPLETED = "completed"; FAILED = "failed"

claim: 12 AgentOS knowledge endpoints
quote: POST /knowledge/content; POST /knowledge/remote-content; PATCH /knowledge/content/{id}; GET /knowledge/content; GET /knowledge/content/{id}; DELETE /knowledge/content/{id}; DELETE /knowledge/content; GET /knowledge/content/{id}/status; POST /knowledge/search; GET /knowledge/config; GET /knowledge/{kid}/sources; GET /knowledge/{kid}/sources/{sid}/files

claim: Hybrid search uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion: RRF(d) = Σ 1/(k + rank)
quote: Fusion combines results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) … RRF(d) = Σ 1/(k + rank)

claim: Default RRF k constant is 60
quote: hybrid_rrf_k=60

claim: Hybrid search supported on PgVector, ChromaDB, LanceDB, Weaviate, Milvus, Pinecone
quote: PgVector, ChromaDB, LanceDB, Weaviate, Milvus, Pinecone

claim: Agentic RAG enabled by Agent(search_knowledge=True) which exposes search_knowledge_base() tool
quote: set `search_knowledge=True` to add a `search_knowledge_base()` tool

claim: Traditional RAG enabled by add_knowledge_to_context=True (auto-injects)
quote: set `add_knowledge_to_context=True` to automatically add references from the knowledge base to the Agent's context

claim: PgVector positioned for production up to 1M docs
quote: PgVector: Production up to 1M docs, need SQL

claim: skip_if_exists is the biggest reingestion speedup
quote: skip_if_exists=True,  # Don't reprocess existing files

claim: Contents DB is optional; tracks metadata, status, deletion sync
quote: Contents Database is an optional component that tracks what you've added to your knowledge base

claim: AgentKnowledge silently ignores chunking_strategy in v1.4.5
quote: the AgentKnowledge component is skipping the provided chunking_strategy and defaulting to FixedSizeChunking in all cases

claim: Upsert bails on missing row; TODO admits this is wrong
quote: we shouldn't check for content here, we should trust the upsert method to handle conflicts

claim: Multimodal RAG is an open gap
quote: with GPT-5.2 the model frequently hallucinates or drifts when handling these multimodal tool outputs

claim: 10000x benchmark measures only client object instantiation
quote: This '10,000x' faster claim is specific to how long it takes to instantiate a client object, before actually interacting with it

claim: Agno relies on third-party platforms for observability (no native LangSmith equivalent)
quote: Agno integrates with third-party platforms like AgentOps and Langfuse

claim: 40,205 stars, 5,394 forks, 921 open issues on 2026-05-19
quote: {"stars":40205,"forks":5394,"open_issues":921}

claim: Benchmark methodology: tracemalloc, 1000 runs, Apple M4, Oct 2025
quote: To measure memory usage, the tracemalloc library is used … running the Agent 1000 times … October 2025 on an Apple M4 MacBook Pro

claim: Cookbook basic_rag uses Qdrant + OpenAIEmbedder + Knowledge.ainsert + Agent(add_knowledge_to_context=True)
quote: Knowledge(vector_db=Qdrant(collection="basic_rag", url=qdrant_url, search_type=SearchType.hybrid, embedder=OpenAIEmbedder(id="text-embedding-3-small"))); Agent(model=OpenAIResponses(id="gpt-5.2"), knowledge=knowledge, add_knowledge_to_context=True, search_knowledge=False)

claim: AgentOS knowledge example demonstrates two-DB pattern (PgVector + PostgresDb)
quote: Knowledge(vector_db=PgVector(...), contents_db=sync_documents_db)

claim: Metadata filters combine with AND; agentic filters via enable_agentic_knowledge_filters
quote: Multiple filters are combined with AND logic. enable_agentic_knowledge_filters=True

claim: AgentOS filter operators: EQ/GT/LT/IN + AND/OR/NOT; invalid filters warn rather than error
quote: Comparison: EQ, GT, LT, IN — Logical: AND, OR, NOT — Invalid filters are gracefully ignored with warnings

claim: Agno was renamed from phidata; current repo agno-agi/agno; old phidata repo archived
quote: github.com/agno-agi/phidata

claim: AgenticChunking defaults to OpenAIChat with DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL_ID
quote: falls back to OpenAIChat(DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL_ID); custom_prompt support added Mar 2026 (#7085)

claim: SemanticChunking exposes 13 tunables incl. similarity_threshold=0.5, similarity_window=3
quote: similarity_threshold=0.5, similarity_window=3, filter_polyorder=3

claim: Recent commit themes (last 6mo): cloud-source ingestion, hardening, new readers
quote: Azure Blob #6120, Remote support #6106, CodeChunking #5981, Docling #6981, LLMsTxtReader #7458, SSRF guards #7892
